Cuphea baccifera is a flowering plant that belongs to the Lythraceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, and aid in wound healing.

The bioactive constituents of Cuphea baccifera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These compounds have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities.

The most common herbal preparations made with Cuphea baccifera include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, and aid in wound healing.
